Your most critical pumps are the ones you can’t see

Submersible pumps, borehole pumps, sewage pumps. Your highest-risk assets sit where sensors can’t go. When they fail, you find out through a pollution event, a regulatory fine, or a flooded site.

SAM4 monitors pump health from the motor control cabinet using Electrical Signature Analysis. No sensors in the wet well. No confined-space entry. Install in 60–90 minutes per asset.

£20M+ documented benefits • 5,000+ pumps monitored across water and wastewater.

Request a Demo How We Help

Trusted by leaders in water and wastewater

The problem

Water assets fail silently. And expensively

Water and wastewater utilities operate thousands of pump sets across distributed networks. Most run unmonitored between scheduled maintenance rounds. The consequences of an undetected fault range from service interruption to environmental prosecution.

Sewage pump blockages

Blockages cause overflows and pollution incidents. By the time operators respond, the spill has already happened. SAM4 detects blockage presence, location, and severity in real time. Before the overflow.

Submerged asset access

Pulling a borehole pump for inspection costs thousands and takes the asset offline for days. SAM4 reads the electrical signature from the MCC. No crane. No diver. No sensors on the asset.

Ageing infrastructure & regulatory risk

Degrading rotors, worn seals, and failing bearings across treatment works and pumping stations go unnoticed until they trigger environmental incidents and six-figure fines. Ofwat ties performance commitments to pollution prevention. Unplanned failures create compliance exposure that reactive maintenance cannot manage.

Energy waste hiding in plain sight

Pumping accounts for up to 90% of a water utility’s electricity bill. Oversized pumps, throttled valves, and degraded impellers waste energy around the clock. Without per-asset power data, the losses stay invisible. SAM4 measures real-time energy consumption per pump, so you can pinpoint waste and act on it.

What we monitor

Critical assets in water and wastewater

SAM4 monitors any AC motor-driven rotating equipment from the motor control cabinet. In water and wastewater, the most common assets include:

Pumps

Submersible pumps, borehole pumps, booster pumps, sewage pumps, and process pumps. SAM4 detects blockages, cavitation, bearing degradation, seal wear, and efficiency loss.

Learn more →

Blowers & fans

Aeration blowers and ventilation fans in treatment works. SAM4 tracks bearing condition, energy performance, and mechanical degradation.

Learn more →

Agitators & mixers

Oxidation ditch rotors, mixers, and scrapers. SAM4 provides early warning of mechanical faults that visual inspection cannot catch.

Learn more →

Proof

Water & Wastewater case studies

£748k Losses prevented

Southern Water: £748k in losses prevented

SAM4 detected three developing faults: bearing degradation on a return activated sludge pump, a seal failure on a storm pump, and a coupling issue on an inlet works pump. All caught weeks before unplanned shutdowns.

Read case study →
£390k Fines avoided

Yorkshire Water: £390k in potential fines avoided

Early alerts on degrading assets prevented pollution events that would have triggered significant regulatory penalties. Yorkshire Water now monitors 1,000+ pumping stations with SAM4.

Read case study →
7% Efficiency increase

Vitens: 7% operational efficiency increase

Real-time pump performance data helped Vitens optimise operations and reduce energy waste across their network.

Read case study →

How it installs

No sensors in the wet well

SAM4 installs inside the motor control cabinet. No sensors in the wet well. No confined-space entry. No disruption to pumping operations.

  1. Install in the MCC Clip-on current and voltage sensors attach to existing wiring in the motor control cabinet. Takes 60–90 minutes per asset.
  2. Connect to SAM4 cloud The DAQ device samples the electrical signal at 20 kHz and sends data securely to the SAM4 platform for AI-driven analysis.
  3. Get actionable alerts Receive predictive alerts weeks to months before failure, with fault type, severity, and recommended action.
See how it installs →

From your first pumping station to every site in your network

ESA installs at the MCC. Once your hardest-to-reach submersible pumps are covered, scaling to the rest of the fleet is a configuration change, not a new project. SAM4 catches developing faults (reactive savings) and identifies efficiency losses (proactive optimisation). The same signal. The same installation.

Start Your Asset Audit Request a Demo

Not ready for a full deployment? Start with a SAM4-X validation: 1–10 assets, 90 days, real results.
